title: FINRA Case 25-01803 — Rebecca Haubry v. Robinhood Financial, LLC
text:
Award
FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
In the Matter of the Arbitration Between:
Claimant Case Number: 25-01803
Rebecca Haubry
vs.
Respondent Hearing Site: Seattle, Washington
Robinhood Financial, LLC
Awards are rendered by independent arbitrators who are chosen by the parties to issue final,
binding decisions. FINRA makes available an arbitration forum—pursuant to rules approved by
the SEC—but has no part in deciding the award.
Nature of the Dispute: Customer vs. Member
This matter proceeded pursuant to Rule 12800 of the Code of Arbitration Procedure (“Code”)
under the Special Proceeding option.
The evidentiary hearing was conducted by videoconference.
REPRESENTATION OF PARTIES
Claimant Rebecca Haubry (“Claimant”) appeared pro se.
For Respondent Robinhood Financial, LLC (“Respondent”): Benjamin A. Schloss, Esq., Turner
Dhillon LLP, Los Angeles, California.
CASE INFORMATION
Statement of Claim filed on or about: August 26, 2025.
Claimant signed the Submission Agreement: August 26, 2025.
Statement of Answer filed by Respondent on or about: October 16, 2025.
Respondent signed the Submission Agreement: October 8, 2025.
CASE SUMMARY
In the Statement of Claim, Claimant asserted the following causes of action: negligence; breach
of fidicuary duty; breach of FINRA Rules; and failure to supervise. The causes of action relate to
unspecified securities and cryptocurrency.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-01803
Award Page 2 of 5
Unless specifically admitted in the Statement of Answer, Respondent denied the allegations made
in the Statement of Claim and asserted various affirmative defenses.
RELIEF REQUESTED
In the Statement of Claim, Claimant requested:
1. Damages of no less than $30,000.00;
2. Lost opportunity costs and future gains;
3. Reimbursement of filing fees;
4. Compensation for stress and time lost;
5. Interest, costs, and attorneys’ fees as allowed; and
6. Any further relief deemed just and proper.
In the Statement of Answer, Respondent requested:
1. Claimant’s claims be denied in their entirety;
2. An award be entered in favor of Respondent;
3. Claimant be required to pay all costs and non-attorneys’ fees incurred in these
proceedings; and
4. Such other and further relief as the Arbitrator deems appropriate.
OTHER ISSUES CONSIDERED AND DECIDED
The Arbitrator acknowledges having read the pleadings and other materials filed by the parties.
The Arbitrator has provided an explanation of the decision in this award. The explanation is for
the parties’ information only and is not precedential in nature.
FINDINGS
Claimant is not an experienced investor. She received an inheritance of $30,000 and invested it
with Respondent on July 27, 2020. Respondent's firm's business model is to permit clients to
direct the investment of the client's own funds. Respondent does not employ financial advisors.
On August 19, 2025, Claimant received a call from a person identifying himself as a technical
support person of Respondent. The caller asked Claimant to repeatedly confirm that she was
the owner of various investments in her portfolio. Claimant did so. In fact, the caller was a
scammer. Each time that Claimant confirmed that she was the owner of her various
investments, the caller was able to label her response as an affirmative answer to his question
seeking permission to sell that investment and invest it in cryptocurrency. Claimant looked at her
account statement and saw that her investments were disappearing. Claimant and Respondent
agree that Claimant was the victim of a third-party scam, of which both were unaware. After the
scam, Claimant immediately called Respondent and reported the disappearances. Respondent
was unable to reverse the sales and re-investments in cryptocurrency. Claimant lost the entirety
of her $30,000 investment.
In essence, Respondent is an order-facilitator. It gives no advice to its clients, who choose the
firm because they want to direct their own investments. Both parties agree that Claimant was

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-01803
Award Page 3 of 5
the innocent and unfortunate victim of a scam, but Respondent had nothing to do with the scam
and could not unwind it.
AWARD
After considering the pleadings, the testimony and evidence presented at the hearing, the
Arbitrator has decided in full and final resolution of the issues submitted for determination as
follows:
1. Claimant’s claims are denied in their entirety.
2. Any and all claims for relief not specifically addressed herein, including any requests for
attorneys’ fees, are denied.
FEES
Pursuant to the Code, the following fees are assessed:
Filing Fees
FINRA Dispute Resolution Services assessed a filing fee* for each claim:
Initial Claim Filing Fee =$ 600.00
*The filing fee is made up of a non-refundable and a refundable portion.
Member Fees
Member fees are assessed to each member firm that is a party in these proceedings or to the
member firm(s) that employed the associated person(s) at the time of the event(s) giving rise to
the dispute. Accordingly, as a party, Respondent is assessed the following:
Member Surcharge =$ 975.00
Member Process Fee =$ 2,275.00
Postponement Fees
Postponements granted during these proceedings for which fees were assessed or waived:
March 12, 2026, postponement requested by parties Waived
Total Postponement Fees Waived
Hearing Session Fees and Assessments
The Arbitrator has assessed hearing session fees for each session conducted. A session is any
meeting between the parties and the Arbitrator, including a pre-hearing conference with the
Arbitrator, which lasts four (4) hours or less. Fees associated with these proceedings are:
One (1) pre-hearing session with a single Arbitrator @ $450.00/session =$ 450.00
Pre-Hearing Conference: December 8, 2025 1 session

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-01803
Award Page 4 of 5
One (1) hearing session @ $450.00/session =$ 450.00
Hearing: March 19, 2026 1 session
Total Hearing Session Fees =$ 900.00
The Arbitrator has assessed the total hearing session fees to Claimant.
All balances are payable to FINRA Dispute Resolution Services and are due upon receipt.

FINRA Dispute Resolution Services
Arbitration No. 25-01803
Award Page 5 of 5
ARBITRATOR
Stephany Adriene Watson - Sole Public Arbitrator
I, the undersigned Arbitrator, do hereby affirm that I am the individual described herein and who
executed this instrument, which is my award.
Arbitrator's Signature
